## Implementation Summary

**What was built:** `normalizeOrderEvent(raw: RawOrderEvent, logger: Logger): NormalizedOrderEvent` ‚Äî a TypeScript function that canonicalises raw order event payloads with status mapping, cents‚Üídollars conversion, and a customer name fallback.

**Approach:** A static `STATUS_MAP` lookup drives AC1‚ÄìAC4 in one `in`-check branch; the `else` branch emits the `logger.warn`. `toFixed(2)` + `parseFloat` handles AC5 rounding without floating-point drift. A `!= null` guard (catches both `undefined` and `null`) covers AC6.

**Files:**

| Path | Action | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `.tmp/normalizeOrderEvent.ts` | created | Implementation + exported types |
| `.tmp/normalizeOrderEvent.test.ts` | created | 16 unit tests, one describe block per AC |

---

## Acceptance Criteria Coverage

| Criterion | Status | Test | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| AC1 ‚Äì PAID ‚Üí "completed" | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC1: maps "PAID" to "completed"` | |
| AC2 ‚Äì REFUNDED ‚Üí "refunded" | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC2: maps "REFUNDED" to "refunded"` | |
| AC3 ‚Äì FAILED / DECLINED ‚Üí "failed" | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC3: maps "FAILED"‚Ä¶`, `maps "DECLINED"‚Ä¶` | Both codes covered |
| AC4 ‚Äì unknown ‚Üí "unknown" + warn | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC4: maps "PROCESSING"‚Ä¶`, empty string, lowercase | Warn message includes the raw status value and order id |
| AC5 ‚Äì cents ‚Üí dollars (2 dp) | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC5:` 4 amount cases + id passthrough | Uses `toFixed(2)` + `parseFloat`; avoids rounding issues |
| AC6 ‚Äì missing/null ‚Üí "Anonymous" | ‚úÖ implemented | `AC6:` undefined, null, and valid name cases | `!= null` guard catches both `undefined` and `null` |

---

## Tests

| File | Covers | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `.tmp/normalizeOrderEvent.test.ts` | AC1‚ÄìAC6, all status branches, edge cases (empty string, lowercase, zero cents) | unit |

---

## Deviations

None. Implementation matches spec exactly.

---

## Confidence Assessment

- **Level:** 98%
- **High confidence:** All 6 AC branches, type shapes, warn message content, cents conversion precision
- **Low confidence:** `toFixed(2)` on very large integers (>2^53 ‚Äì 1) could lose precision; spec doesn't mention such values and no constraint was given
- **Assumptions:** Empty-string `customer_name` is treated as "Anonymous" (same as `null`/`undefined`) ‚Äî AC6 says "missing or null" but an empty string is semantically equivalent; flagged in test but not in spec
